pages.text: It was Makar Sankranti, and the sky was full of colorful kites. <name> held his favorite kite, bright orange with shiny tassels. He ran across the terrace, letting it soar higher and higher.
Suddenly — *whoosh!* — the wind snatched the kite, and it flew away! “Oh no!” cried <name>. “My kite!”

He ran through the sunny lanes, hopping over puddles and laughing at the fluttering tassels. In the next lane, the milkman on his bicycle stopped. “Looking for this?” he asked, pointing to the kite tangled on a wire. “Almost!” said <name>, “but I need to catch it!”
A ginger cat purred and darted ahead, showing him a shortcut through flower-filled yards. Then, kind Uncle Ravi zoomed by on his scooter. “Need a lift?” he said with a smile.
Up rooftops, across fields, and through bustling streets, <name> chased his kite, giggling all the way. Finally, on the last terrace near his house, he leapt — and caught the kite in his hands!

Breathing hard but happy, <name> looked around. “Wow… my adventure was right here in my own neighborhood!” He flew the kite again, higher than before, and shared sweets with neighbors, feeling proud and joyful.
And <name> learned that sometimes, the best adventures are just outside your door! 🎏✨
